This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command can start background processes using `&`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      Stderr: Output on stderr stream. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command can start background processes using `&`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      The following information is returned:

      Command: Executed command.
      Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
      Stdout: Output on stdout stream. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Stderr: Output on stderr stream. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
      Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
      Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.
      Background PIDs: List of background processes started or `(none)`.
      Process Group PGID: Process group started or `(none)`

args.command:
cd /Users/benny/Mission-Forge/issues && cat > /tmp/create_remaining_issues.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash

issues=(
  "schemas-validators.md"
  "git-test-utilities.md"
  "workspace-commands.md"
  "decompose-command.md"
  "plan-command.md"
  "next-command.md"
  "baseline-flow.md"
  "sub-mission-validation.md"
  "parent-validation.md"
  "report-generation.md"
  "bob-skill-slash-commands.md"
  "demo-mission-prep.md"
  "board-backend.md"
  "board-frontend.md"
  "mission-detail-panel.md"
  "report-renderer.md"
  "dependency-graph-view.md"
  "cli-wrapper-endpoints.md"
  "watsonx-mission-chat.md"
  "stakeholder-translation.md"
  "demo-polish-submission.md"
)

cd /Users/benny/Mission-Forge/issues

for issue_file in "${issues[@]}"; do
  title=$(grep -m 1 "^# " "$issue_file" | sed 's/^# //')
  echo "Creating: $title"
  gh issue create --title "$title" --body-file "$issue_file" --repo loudiman/Mission-Forge
  sleep 1
done

echo ""
echo "All 22 issues created successfully!"
gh issue list --repo loudiman/Mission-Forge --limit 25 --json number,title --jq '.[] | "#\(.number): \(.title)"'
EOF
chmod +x /tmp/create_remaining_issues.sh
/tmp/create_remaining_issues.sh

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command can start background processes using `&`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      The following information is returned:

      Command: Executed command.
      Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
      Stdout: Output on stdout stream. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Stderr: Output on stderr stream. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
      Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
      Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.
      Background PIDs: List of background processes started or `(none)`.
      Process Group PGID: Process group started or `(none)`

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command can start background processes using `&`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      The following information is returned:

      Command: Executed command.
      Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
      Stdout: Output on stdout stream. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Stderr: Output on stderr stream. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
      Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
      Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.
      Background PIDs: List of background processes started or `(none)`.
      Process Group PGID: Process group started or `(none)`

  sleep 1
done && echo "" && echo "✓ All 22 issues created!" && gh issue list --repo loudiman/Mission-Forge --limit 25 --json number,title --jq '.[] | "#\(.number): \(.title)"'

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command can start background processes using `&`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      The following information is returned:

      Command: Executed command.
      Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
      Stdout: Output on stdout stream. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Stderr: Output on stderr stream. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
      Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
      Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.
      Background PIDs: List of background processes started or `(none)`.
      Process Group PGID: Process group started or `(none)`
